title: Dopplegänger

'''Dopplegänger''', also known as '''''Similfuturus''''' in [[High Galactic (language)|High Galactic]], was an advanced [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] that allowed a user to create a short-lived duplicate of themselves or an external object that was visually indistinguishable from the real item through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

==Description==
Dopplegänger, also known as ''Similfuturus'' in [[High Galactic (language)|High Galactic]], was an advanced [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] that allowed a user to create a short-lived duplicate of themselves or an external object that was visually indistinguishable from the real item through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Those who perfected this ability could create [[Force phantom]]s of any person of their choosing or trick an enemy into seeing more objects, such as [[Droid/Legends|droids]], than were actually present.<ref name="Jedi Path" />

==Applications==
The [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force user]] could perceive all of the dopplegänger's senses, and to those interacting with it, the dopplegänger seemed real. The user could even interact with the world using [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] to move objects, furthering the believability of the illusion.

The dopplegänger could also be detected by all droid audio and video sensors and would fade away if the Force user was injured and ceased using the power.

Sometimes even a powerful Sith Lord might think that the projection was real.<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Inferno">''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]''</ref> Moreover, the application of conjuring a duplicate to be a target for an attack could be quite effective, though only against a single agressive action.<ref>''[[Dawn of the Jedi: Force War 2]]''</ref><ref name="Legacy of the Force: Inferno" />

[[Force phantom]] was similar to a dopplegänger, in that it was an astral projection of a being created through the Force, but it was a projection of something other than the user's own image. Force phantoms were most likely used only by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], and usually exhibited corporeal properties and inflicted damage on living beings.

==Users==
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]],<ref name="Dark Empire 3">[[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]]</ref><ref name="Dark Empire Sourcebook">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]],<ref name="Star Wars: The New Droid Army">''[[Star Wars: The New Droid Army]]''</ref> and [[Tau (individual)|Xesh]]<ref name="Dawn of the Jedi: The Prisoner of Bogan 4">[[Dawn of the Jedi: The Prisoner of Bogan 4|''Dawn of the Jedi: The Prisoner of Bogan'' 4]]</ref> all used this ability.

[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] once wondered if [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]] had used this power to trick him into making a foolish attack during training, as indeed was the case. Later both Sith would meditate for months, using this power to join as one dark body of light and fly the Force in a ploy to take over the powers of the galexy.<ref name="Darth Plagueis novel">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> A pernicious use of this art was when Lord Wyyrlok used this power to trick a cult into nearly exterminating itself.<ref>''[[Legacy (2006) 27]]''</ref>

A [[Image|distinct version]] of dopplegänger was also used by the [[Fallanassi/Legends|Fallanassi]], which allowed the creation of a perfect illusion of himself, or another object, that was visually indistinguishable from what was real. This illusion was not limited in size and could be both seen and heard, but, unlike other versions, lacked the ability to physically move objects via telekinesis.<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref>

The [[Padawan/Legends|Jedi Padawan]] [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] wondered if this ability would work well on a whole army of clones in her annotations of ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''.<ref name="Path" /> At times this art was used to spawn into being an insubstantial copy of an object.<ref name="I, Jedi">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref><ref name="Path">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>  Some Jedi used a version of this power to make disguises of themselves.<ref name="Shield of Lies">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref><ref name="Jedi Academy Training Manual">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The German spelling is "Doppelgänger," but the term is sometimes spelled as "doppelganger." In [[1993]]'s ''[[Star Wars Chess]]'', Yoda (two of whom serve as the rooks) casts two doppelgängers of himself to confuse [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] before defeating the Sith Lord.<ref name="Chess">''[[Star Wars Chess]]''</ref>
